body:
Nancy — circling back on the 17 September install. The freight elevator is booked 8:00 to 14:00. Hooks and the new T-bar are in. We'll need final piece order by Monday the 13th so I can mark the wall.

Two questions:
1. Do you want the triptych panels grouped on the north wall or split across the entry corner? I keep going back and forth.
2. The brass plaques — initials only, or full title underneath? Marisol used full titles for the Brooklyn show.

We can do the walk-through Tuesday at 10 if that works. Kim
snippet: Circling back on the 17 September install. Freight elevator booked 8 to 14.
subject: Ironwork solo — install schedule, 17 September

body:
Kim — Tuesday at 10 works. I'll bring the piece order and a printed wall map.

On the two questions:
1. North wall, grouped. The corner split worked at Sable & Thread because the room was longer; here the eye needs the panels together.
2. Full titles. The Brooklyn lesson was that people lingered.

Currently at eight finished. Three more in the studio queue this month. Two are still on the wall waiting on whether they earn their place. Nancy
snippet: Tuesday at 10 works. Bringing piece order and printed wall map.
subject: Re: Ironwork solo — install schedule, 17 September

body:
Hi Nancy — the second deposit goes out this Friday per the contract schedule. AP will email the remittance. Facilities also asked me to get a crating quote for the lobby install in July 2027; do you have a preferred freight art handler?

We're holding 31 July 2027 firm. The lobby refurb finishes the week prior. Dana
snippet: Second deposit goes Friday. Need a freight art handler for the July 2027 lobby install.
subject: Silverline triptych — second deposit + crating quote

body:
Dana — thanks. Confirming receipt of the schedule.

For the crate, I'd use Atelier 4 (Philadelphia). They handled the Sable & Thread shipment last year and the panels arrived without a scratch. I'll get them to send you a direct quote — should land in your inbox by next Friday.

31 July is on the wall in my studio. Nancy
snippet: Atelier 4 in Philadelphia — they handled Sable & Thread. Quote coming next Friday.
subject: Re: Silverline triptych — second deposit + crating quote

body:
Hello Nancy and Devon — quick check-in. Your mother had a quieter morning today; she finished breakfast and sat in the sun room with the Sunday paper for about an hour.

She asked twice when Nancy was coming next, which is the most engaged she's been on names this week. I told her the 24th. Please let me know if anything changes on your end.

Carla Mendez, RN — Meadowlark Floor 2
snippet: Ruth had a quieter morning. Sun room about an hour. Asked twice about Nancy's next visit.
subject: Sunday — Ruth had a quieter morning

body:
Carla — thank you. The 24th is on. I'll get in Saturday afternoon and stay through Sunday lunch.

If she's having a sun-room sort of week, I'll bring the small format album from the Brooklyn show — she liked the pages last time even if the names slip. Nancy
snippet: The 24th is on. Saturday afternoon through Sunday lunch.
subject: Re: Sunday — Ruth had a quieter morning

body:
Order #BBFA-22841 confirmed. Shipping to 823 Frankford Ave, Unit 4A, Philadelphia PA 19125.

1× Mijello Fusion 24-well palette
2× Stonehenge Aqua Cold Press, 22 × 30, pack of 10
1× Sennelier ink, sepia
1× Da Vinci Maestro round, size 8

Subtotal $186.40. Below the studio threshold so we proceeded as agreed. Tracking on dispatch.
snippet: Order confirmed. Mijello 24-well, Stonehenge cold press, Sennelier sepia, Da Vinci size 8.
subject: Studio supplies — Mijello holders + Stonehenge restock

body:
Did you see the deadline shifted to 14 December? That gives you breathing room after Ironwork opens.

I still think the Comporta one is the better fit even if Lisbon sounds more glamorous. The light alone. Margaux
snippet: Deadline shifted to 14 December. Comporta still the better fit.
subject: Re: Portugal residency — application thread

body:
I saw. Two weeks after the opening — that's exactly the gap where I'll either be ready to apply or completely empty. We'll see which.

Agreed on Comporta. The Lisbon studio share is too social, and you know how I do with social right after a show. Nancy
snippet: Two weeks after the opening — either ready or completely empty. We'll see.
subject: Re: Re: Portugal residency — application thread

body:
Nancy — two Foundations II students have submitted late-critique petitions for the spring semester. Mai Nguyen and Jordan Park.

Per faculty handbook 4.2 we need your sign-off before Friday. Both have documentation. Forms attached. Sarah
snippet: Two Foundations II late-critique petitions. Mai Nguyen and Jordan Park. Sign-off by Friday.
subject: Foundations II — student late critique requests

body:
Sarah — approved both. Mai's documentation is on the housing situation we discussed in February; Jordan's is the medical leave from October. Forms signed and back to you this afternoon.

Grades land before May 4 regardless. Nancy
snippet: Approved both. Forms signed and back this afternoon.
subject: Re: Foundations II — student late critique requests

body:
Nancy — quick inventory note. I have three of yours on the floor: 'River Glyphs II', 'Counterweight (Iron)', and the smaller 'Brass Field — Studies'. The Brass Field sold Saturday to a regular collector; cheque clears next week.

Want me to pull a replacement off the storage rack or hold the slot for something newer? Marisol
snippet: Brass Field sold Saturday. Pull a replacement or hold the slot for newer work?
subject: Sable & Thread — Brooklyn group show inventory check

body:
Marisol — congratulations on the sale.

Hold the slot. I have a piece on the wall that wants another two weeks; if it lands I'll send it up before the end of the month. If it doesn't, pull 'River Glyphs III' from storage. Nancy
snippet: Hold the slot. Piece on the wall wants two more weeks. Else pull River Glyphs III.
subject: Re: Sable & Thread — Brooklyn group show inventory check

body:
Nancy — I have the March books ready. Three line items I want to flag before the quarterly:

1. Silverline second deposit — needs a 1099 follow-up.
2. The studio supply spend is up 18% YoY; expected given Ironwork prep but worth seeing on paper.
3. The Sable & Thread sale net — Marisol's commission cleared correctly.

Free Thursday at 10 if you can make the office. 1635 Market. Nina
snippet: March books ready. Silverline 1099 follow-up, supply spend up 18%, S&T net cleared.
subject: Quarterly invoice review — March books

body:
Nina — Thursday at 10 works. Studio block ends at 13 so I'll come straight from there.

On the 18% supply spend: that tracks with the panel-prep order in February. The next big buy is the framing budget for Ironwork, which we discussed in the January call. Nancy
snippet: Thursday at 10. Supply spend tracks with February panel prep. Framing budget next.
subject: Re: Quarterly invoice review — March books

body:
Hey Nancy — quick status on the portfolio site rebuild. Three open items before we can flip the DNS:

1. Image gallery still pre-loads thumbnails in the wrong order on Safari. Working on it.
2. The mobile menu loses the 'Press' link when you tap fast. Probably a stale event handler.
3. Stripe test mode is wired up for the print editions but I want one more dry run before we go live.

Nothing urgent — just keeping the list visible. Owen
snippet: Three open items: Safari thumbnail order, mobile menu Press link, Stripe dry run.
subject: Portfolio site — bug list before launch

body:
Owen — thank you. None of this is urgent for the Ironwork window — the site doesn't need to be the announcement channel; the gallery's email is.

Take your time on the Stripe dry run. I'd rather it work for one customer than partially for ten. Nancy
snippet: None of this is urgent for Ironwork. Site doesn't need to be the announcement channel.
subject: Re: Portfolio site — bug list before launch

body:
Nancy — Rittenhouse Internal Medicine here. We have your annual physical scheduling window open for November.

Mornings or afternoons preferred? Per the usual, we'd want fasting labs the morning prior. Reply with three windows and we'll lock one. Frank
snippet: Annual physical scheduling window open for November. Three windows please.
subject: Dr. Moss — annual physical, November scheduling

body:
Frank — three windows, afternoons (studio block is mornings):

Tuesday 9 November, 14:00
Tuesday 16 November, 14:00
Thursday 18 November, 14:00

Any of those works. Nancy
snippet: Three afternoon windows on the 9th, 16th, 18th.
subject: Re: Dr. Moss — annual physical, November scheduling

body:
Sardine Room Thursday, usual time? I have to be back in the studio by 22:00 because the new piece is finally drying in the right direction and I don't want to break the streak.

Also — the gold leaf you showed me on Saturday. Was that the Sepp loose-leaf or the Manetti? Theo
snippet: Sardine Room Thursday usual time. Sepp or Manetti on the gold leaf?
subject: Theo dinner — Thursday at The Sardine Room?

body:
Sardine Room Thursday. I'll book for 19:30 so we're done by 21:30 and you can get back.

Manetti. The Sepp is too aggressive against the umber ground; the Manetti settles instead of arguing. Nancy
snippet: Sardine Room 19:30 Thursday. Manetti, not Sepp.
subject: Re: Theo dinner — Thursday at The Sardine Room?

body:
Nancy — confirming your fall '27 load: Foundations II (Tuesday/Thursday 10–13) and the Materials Practice seminar (Thursday 14–16). Sam will pick up Foundations I.

Office hours are yours to set; I'd recommend keeping them off Wednesdays per your standing studio block. Margaret
snippet: Fall '27 load: Foundations II + Materials Practice seminar. Sam on Foundations I.
subject: Harrington dean's office — fall course load confirmation

body:
Margaret — confirmed and thank you for protecting the Wednesday morning. Office hours will be Tuesday 13:30–15:00 and Thursday 16:00–17:00.

I'll send the materials list for both courses by 1 August. Nancy
snippet: Confirmed. Office hours Tuesday 13:30–15:00, Thursday 16:00–17:00.
subject: Re: Harrington dean's office — fall course load confirmation

body:
Nancy — your annual is coming due in January. We have Thursday the 14th at 11:00 or Friday the 22nd at 14:00 on the calendar.

Reminder that you mentioned the close work was getting harder last visit; I'd like to test that under the new lighting setup. Dr Whiting
snippet: Two slots. Want to test close-work under the new lighting setup.
subject: Eye exam — January slot

body:
Friday 22 January at 14:00. Thursday is teaching.

Yes to the close-work test. The gold leaf work in particular has me holding pieces too close lately. Nancy
snippet: Friday 22 January 14:00. Close-work test welcome.
subject: Re: Eye exam — January slot

body:
Hey — Meadowlark wants a med review next Tuesday with the on-call geriatric NP. I can do the call but I want you on the line for the cognition portion since you saw her two weeks ago and I haven't been up since March.

11:00 Tuesday? Devon
snippet: Med review next Tuesday 11:00. Want you on the line for the cognition portion.
subject: Devon — Mom's medication review next week

body:
Tuesday 11:00 works — I'll be done with my Tuesday teaching by then.

Two things from my visit: she repeated the Pittsburgh story three times in an hour, and she's been resisting the afternoon walks. Both worth raising. Nancy
snippet: Tuesday 11:00 works. Pittsburgh story repeated, resisting afternoon walks.
subject: Re: Devon — Mom's medication review next week

body:
Nancy — for the joint demo on Thursday I prepped 22 packets (one per Foundations II student plus four extras). Each has the cold-press sample, the two ink trials, and the gum eraser. I left them in your studio cubby.

If you want, I can do the wet-into-wet portion and let you handle the line work. Sam
snippet: 22 packets prepped in your cubby. I can do wet-into-wet, you handle line work.
subject: Class supplies — Foundations II demo packets

body: Sam — packet plan is perfect, thank you. Yes to the split: you on wet-into-wet, me on line work. Let's leave fifteen minutes at the end for student questions; the cohort this term is shyer than usual and the silence after the demo always lasts too long. Nancy
snippet: Yes to the split. Fifteen min at the end for student questions.
subject: Re: Class supplies — Foundations II demo packets

body:
Nancy — per your request, our estimate for the three-panel triptych (estimated 48" × 60" each, oil + mixed media on cradled birch):

Custom T-frame travel crates, foam-lined, climate-stable: $1,840
Pickup at studio, delivery to Silverline Corp, 1700 Market St: $620
On-site uncrating + install supervision: $480

Total $2,940. Estimate good 60 days. Atelier 4
snippet: Three T-frame crates, pickup, delivery, install supervision: $2,940 total.
subject: Atelier 4 — crating estimate for Silverline lobby install

body: Confirming estimate received. I'll forward to Dana Reyes at Silverline to fold into their facility budget per our contract. Hold the 60-day window. Nancy
snippet: Forwarding to Dana at Silverline. Hold the 60-day window.
subject: Re: Atelier 4 — crating estimate for Silverline lobby install

body: Nancy — the laminated almond ones go quick lately so we're holding two for you Saturday alongside your usual espresso. Pay at the counter as always.
snippet: Holding two laminated almond Saturday. Espresso as usual.
subject: Flour & Stone — Saturday market hold

body:
There's a new figurative painter at Sable & Thread two doors down from where you'd land if you came up. Opening Friday the 28th at 18:00.

Marisol mentioned you. No pressure to come up — but I'd like the company if you can. Margaux
snippet: Sable-adjacent opening Friday the 28th, 18:00. No pressure, but want company.
subject: Brooklyn opening — Margaux flagging upcoming weekend

body: I'll come up Friday afternoon and stay through Saturday. The 28th is in the gap between Foundations and the Ironwork final push so it actually fits. Train at 14:30, dinner whatever you want. Nancy
snippet: Friday afternoon through Saturday. Train at 14:30.
subject: Re: Brooklyn opening — Margaux flagging upcoming weekend

body:
Hello Nancy — your February cleaning is due. We have Tuesday 9 February at 09:00 or Wednesday 17 February at 16:30.

Either works. Dr Pham
snippet: February cleaning. 9 Feb 09:00 or 17 Feb 16:30.
subject: Liberty Dental — February cleaning reminder

body: Wednesday 17 February at 16:30. Studio block holds Tuesday morning. Nancy
snippet: Wednesday 17 February 16:30.

body: Off-topic from the books — the Klimt drawings show opens at the Barnes on the 12th. Members' preview Sunday morning. I have a plus-one and Mike's away. Want to come? Coffee after. Nina
snippet: Klimt drawings members' preview Sunday morning. Plus-one open.
subject: Klimt show at the Barnes — invite from Nina

body: Yes. I haven't seen the drawings since the Vienna show in '15. Sunday after Ruth's call — say 13:00 outside the entrance. Nancy
snippet: Sunday 13:00 outside Barnes entrance. After Ruth's call.
subject: Re: Klimt show at the Barnes — invite from Nina

body:
Nancy — first draft of the collector preview list for the 17 September private. Currently 28 names; capacity is 35.

Two notes:
- I left off the Hartfield estate per your last note. Confirm.
- I added two newer collectors from the spring acquisition list. Approve or remove.

Send me your edits by Friday. Kim
snippet: 28 of 35 collector preview names. Hartfield off, two new on. Edits by Friday.
subject: Ironwork — collector preview invitations draft

body:
Kim — Hartfield off, confirmed.

On the two new names: keep the first, hold the second. The second collector buys quickly and I'd rather build the relationship over two shows than burn through the work. Send the redlined list back to me when you have it. Nancy
snippet: Hartfield off confirmed. Keep first new, hold second.
subject: Re: Ironwork — collector preview invitations draft

body:
Hi Nancy — your unlimited membership renews 1 February at $128/month. Same evening cadence as last year (Monday and Wednesday 18:00 typical).

Reply RENEW to confirm, or CHANGE for plan options. Northern Liberty Yoga
snippet: Renewal 1 February at $128/month. Reply RENEW to confirm.
subject: Yoga studio — Monday/Wednesday membership renewal

body: RENEW. Nancy
snippet: RENEW.
subject: Re: Yoga studio — Monday/Wednesday membership renewal

body:
Nancy — Bartoli's quote came in on the Brass Field series framing. Three options:

A) Oxidised brass float frames, 1.5" reveal — $340 each, 12 pieces, total $4,080
B) Natural walnut box frames, museum glass — $290 each, total $3,480
C) Mix: float frames on the larger pieces, walnut box on the studies — total $3,750

I lean C. Your call. Kim
snippet: Bartoli framing quote. Three options A/B/C. I lean C, your call.
subject: Ironwork — framing decisions for the Brass Field series

body:
Kim — C, with one swap. The two largest Brass Field pieces go in oxidised brass; the four mid-sized switch to walnut box; the studies stay walnut box. That should bring it under $3,600.

$3,600 is above my threshold so I'll confirm again when Bartoli sends the revised quote. Nancy
snippet: Modified C. Largest in oxidised brass, mid in walnut, studies walnut. Above threshold; will reconfirm.
subject: Re: Ironwork — framing decisions for the Brass Field series

body:
Hey — the Stripe dry run cleared. I'm thinking we cut over the DNS Sunday morning at 7. Traffic is basically zero at that hour and if anything breaks I have all day to fix it.

I'll send you a single confirmation text once it's live. Nothing else to do on your end. Owen
snippet: DNS cutover Sunday 7am. Confirmation text once live.
subject: Owen — DNS cutover, low-stakes

body: Coming down Friday night, leaving Sunday after lunch. Want to do the Fishtown market Saturday at 9 like old times? I'll bring the good coffee thermos so we don't have to wait at Flour & Stone. Margaux
snippet: Down Fri night to Sun lunch. Fishtown market Saturday 9. Bringing thermos.
subject: Sunday market — visiting from Brooklyn this weekend

body:
Farmers market followed by food sounds like an absolutely perfect Sunday and I am willing to be a morning person for it. I'll pick up good coffee from the market, you find us somewhere good to eat after — deal?

Can't wait. This is exactly what I needed.

— Margaux
snippet: Farmers market followed by food sounds like an absolutely perfect Sunday and I a...
subject: Re: Weekend plans
